project_specification.project_name: YouTube Subtitle to AI Article Converter - MVP
project_specification.overview: This project builds a web application deployed on Cloudflare Worker. Users input a YouTube video link with subtitles, and the app organizes the subtitle content into well-structured chapters. By default, the original subtitle content is preserved verbatim — only chapter splitting is applied. When users provide custom generation rules, AI transforms the content per those rules. The app supports real-time streaming generation and chapter-level 5W1H structured summaries. The core product goal is to validate the "video content → structured article" automation workflow, providing content creators and learners with an efficient information organization tool.

frontend.framework: Bundled vanilla JS + CSS via esbuild. No SPA framework — bundled modules for clean separation.
frontend.markdown: Lightweight regex parser for chapter rendering
frontend.stream_rendering: SSE streaming DOM rendering (real-time typewriter effect)
frontend.bundling: esbuild bundles src/frontend/ → public/. Worker serves public/ as static assets.

backend.streaming: TransformStream + SSE (Content-Type: text/event-stream) for real-time AI output
backend.tcp_socket: cloudflare:sockets connect() for Webshare proxy tunneling (Free plan compatible)
backend.security: Encrypted secrets via wrangler.toml or Cloudflare dashboard; no key exposure
backend.modularity: Clear module separation — each file has a single responsibility

youtube_module.library: youtubei.js (npm: youtubei.js) — most active Innertube API library
youtube_module.caption_fetch: Innertube API — session-based YouTube subtitle extraction via Transcript API + caption tracks
youtube_module.language_strategy: zh-CN → zh → en (auto fallback chain) Cloudflare TCP Socket connect() + Webshare.io proxy (anti-captcha) Hard-coded subtitle fallback from src/fallback-subtitles/ (100% availability for demo)

providers.default: Gemini (gemini-2.5-flash) — generateContentStream() + generateContent(). The default AI provider for production deployment.
providers.secondary: DeepSeek (deepseek-v4-flash) — OpenAI-compatible chat completions API, 1M context window. Activated via ?provider=deepseek query parameter. No max_tokens limit — model decides output length freely.

ai_module.selection_logic: Query-parameter routing: GET/POST ?provider=deepseek → DeepSeek (requires DEEPSEEK_API_KEY secret); otherwise → Gemini (default). Frontend provides a <select> model picker that appends the parameter to fetch URLs. Falls back to Gemini for unknown provider values.
ai_module.streaming: SSE streaming with sentence-level batching. Tokens are accumulated server-side until a sentence-ending character (。！？\n) or 40-char threshold, then flushed as a single SSE message. Reduces SSE message count from ~1000 to ~50-100 per article. Frontend renders with fadeInUp animation and blinking cursor during streaming.
ai_module.prompt_engineering: Dual-format prompt templates: plain text for Gemini, chat messages for DeepSeek. Two generation modes: (1) chapter-only splitting — subtitle is split into chapters with original wording preserved, no rewriting; (2) rule-driven generation — article is generated strictly following user-provided rules. Default output language is Chinese. If the user specifies a language preference in custom rules, that takes precedence. Same requirements, different transport format per provider.
ai_module.response_format: Article text in Markdown (preserved or AI-generated), defaulting to Chinese; plus 5W1H structured JSON
ai_module.error_handling: Graceful failure with fallback content; provider-specific error messages (DeepSeek/Gemini distinguished in error output)

storage_module.purpose: Session context cache for 5W1H (no re-transmission of full article from client)
storage_module.data_schema: Type-safe structured session object TTL 3600s (auto cleanup, cost-efficient)

infrastructure.proxy_implementation: TCP Socket tunnel via cloudflare:sockets connect() (Worker-compatible)

prerequisites.item: Cloudflare account (for Worker deployment and KV namespace creation)
Google Gemini API Key (free tier, for gemini-2.5-flash — default production provider)
DeepSeek API Key (optional, free tier — enables DeepSeek via ?provider=deepseek)
Webshare.io free proxy account (for bypassing YouTube anti-bot / captcha)
Node.js + npm environment (local development and dependency installation)

core_features.subtitle_extraction: - Supports standard YouTube link parsing (youtube.com and youtu.be formats) - Prefers Simplified Chinese subtitles; falls back to English when Chinese subtitles are unavailable - Direct mode uses youtubei.js Innertube API to pull subtitles directly - Automatically switches to Webshare TCP Socket proxy mode on captcha / anti-bot trigger - Falls back to built-in hard-coded subtitles from src/fallback-subtitles/ when all else fails - Automatically strips subtitle timestamps and irrelevant markers, outputting clean plain text - Extracted subtitles are the precondition for chapter detection (/api/chapters) and article generation (/api/generate)
core_features.article_generation: - Two generation modes based on whether custom rules are provided: - Default mode (no custom rules): Original subtitle content is preserved verbatim. The system detects topic boundaries and organizes text into chapters without modifying any wording. Output is in Chinese unless subtitles are in another language. - Default dialogue formatting: When subtitles contain conversation or interview content, the default mode automatically merges same-topic exchanges into a single chapter and formats each speaker's lines with their name prefix (e.g., "Jen:", "Mark:"). Each speaker turn is rendered on its own line within the chapter. If multiple participants join the discussion, their content is organized by topic and formatted individually with name prefixes. - Custom rule mode (rules provided): The system generates a new article strictly following user requirements such as task type, style, target audience, language, and constraints. - Both modes default to Chinese output; the user can specify a different output language via custom rules - Both modes support real-time streaming text generation via the selected AI provider - Sentence-level batching: output is accumulated until a natural sentence break or reasonable length threshold, then flushed - Frontend renders new content with a smooth fade-in animation as it arrives - A blinking cursor indicator appears during streaming and is automatically removed on completion - Markdown headings are auto-detected for chapter division - Frontend model selector for choosing between available AI providers - Shows loading state during generation; provides friendly degradation on failure
core_features.chapter_detection: - Chapters are detected automatically from ## Markdown headings in the generated article text as it streams - The backend extracts chapter titles from ## headings and stores them in the session for 5W1H queries - Chapter titles are normalized: ## prefix and leading/trailing whitespace stripped before storage and matching - Frontend renders each ## heading as an h2 element with natural line breaks (no border boxes) - After article generation completes, 5W1H action buttons are injected next to each chapter heading - Only content from user-selected chapters is included in the final output when selectedChapters is specified
core_features.chapter_summary: - Automatically splits the article into chapters based on Markdown ## headings - Chapter titles are normalized: ## prefix and leading/trailing whitespace stripped before storage and matching - Clicking the [5W1H] button next to a chapter sends sessionId + chapter title to server - Server retrieves full article context from KV (no client re-transmission) - Generates structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How summaries via the active AI provider (Gemini or DeepSeek) - Summaries rendered in a fixed format: Who, What, When, Where, Why, How as structured JSON - Summary boxes are collapsed by default, expand on click
core_features.ui_interaction: - Clean single-page layout divided into input area and result display area - Input area: YouTube link field (required), custom rules text field (optional), model selector, and "生成文章" (Generate Article) button - Generate button auto-disabled during requests to prevent duplicate submissions - Article content with automatic word wrapping and reading optimization, responsive to screen widths - Chapter headings clearly distinguished from body text with scroll anchoring - 5W1H summary boxes collapsed by default, expand on click
core_features.error_handling: - Invalid link format validation with instant feedback on input errors - Subtitle fallback chain: direct → proxy → hard-coded (workflow never breaks) - Gemini API failures return clear error messages for easy debugging - Auto-rotates through multiple Webshare proxy nodes in proxy mode - KV session caches auto-expire after 1 hour to prevent storage bloat - After generation completes, session context persists in KV — 5W1H queries work across page refreshes for up to 1 hour
core_features.smoke_test: - A smoke test must be run after every code change to verify core functionality is intact before proceeding - The smoke test is a single automated script that covers the critical path end-to-end - Smoke test steps: - 1. Build frontend assets and start the dev server - 2. Verify the HTML page and static assets are served correctly - 3. Verify chapter detection works with the demo video and returns a valid chapter list - 4. Verify default-mode article generation produces streaming output with chapter headings - 5. Verify a 5W1H summary can be queried for a chapter and returns all six fields - 6. Verify error handling: invalid URL returns a clear error, missing parameters return 4xx - If any step fails, the change must be fixed before continuing to the next feature - The smoke test uses the hard-coded subtitle fallback to avoid dependency on external services

kv_namespace.description: Stores session context data to enable the 5W1H summary feature

api_endpoints_summary.pages: - GET / — Serves the frontend HTML page - GET /public/* — Serves bundled static assets (JS, CSS)
api_endpoints_summary.api: - POST /api/chapters — Accepts a YouTube URL, returns detected chapter list with titles and brief summaries - POST /api/generate — Accepts YouTube URL, selected chapter indices, and optional custom rules; returns SSE stream of article text with a session ID header - POST /api/5w1h — Accepts session ID and chapter title; returns structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How summary as JSON

section.name: Input Area
section.element: YouTube video URL input field (required, with URL format validation)
Custom generation rules text field (optional, natural language: task type, style, audience, constraints)
AI model selector dropdown (Gemini 2.5 Flash default, DeepSeek V4 Flash)
"生成文章" (Generate Article) button (extracts subtitles + generates article in one click)

section.name: Result Display Area
section.element: Loading indicator with spinner shown during generation
Streaming article rendering area with Markdown heading (# for title, ## for chapters) and paragraph formatting
Chapters rendered as h2 headings with natural line breaks, no border boxes
"5W1H" button injected next to each chapter heading after article completion
Collapsible structured summary box per chapter — expands on click, shows Who/What/When/Where/Why/How

color_scheme.primary: #2563eb (blue, used for buttons and emphasis elements)

components.component: Rounded border, light gray background, focus highlight
Hover effect, disabled state styling (grayed out)
Light border, padding to distinguish different chapters
Light gray background, visually distinct from chapter content

interaction.trigger: User enters a YouTube link, optionally enters custom rules, and clicks "生成文章"

flow.step: 1. Frontend validates the YouTube URL format inline; shows error on invalid input
2. Frontend sends the YouTube URL and optional custom rules to the server
3. Server extracts the video ID and fetches subtitles through the fallback chain (direct, proxy, hard-coded)
4. Server determines the mode: if no custom rules, preserve original text and only split into chapters; if rules are provided, generate new content following those rules
5. Server creates a session and begins streaming the processed content back to the frontend via SSE
6. Frontend renders article content in real-time: title (h1), chapter headings (h2), and paragraphs
7. When streaming completes, server saves the full article, chapter list, and original subtitle to KV; frontend injects 5W1H buttons next to each chapter heading

interaction.trigger: User clicks the "5W1H" button next to a chapter heading

flow.step: 1. Frontend sends the session ID and chapter title to the server
2. Server retrieves the full article context from the session store
3. Server generates a structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How summary for the chapter using the full article as context
4. Server returns the structured summary as JSON
5. Frontend renders the summary in a collapsible box below the chapter

step.title: Smoke Test (Run After Every Change)
step.tasks: - The smoke test must pass after every code change before proceeding to the next feature - Covers: build and dev server startup, static asset serving, chapter detection, default-mode generation, 5W1H summary, and error handling - Uses hard-coded subtitle fallback to ensure fast and reliable execution without external dependencies

step.title: Project Initialization & Foundation
step.tasks: - Initialize npm project with package.json, tsconfig.json, and Cloudflare Worker types - Create wrangler.toml with KV namespace binding, compatibility_date, and esbuild config - Create build.mjs for frontend bundling via esbuild - Install dependencies: youtubei.js (dev: wrangler, esbuild, typescript, @cloudflare/workers-types) - Verify wrangler dev --port 34997 starts without errors - Set up src/backend/ and src/frontend/ directory structure

step.title: YouTube Subtitle Extraction Module
step.tasks: - Verify youtubei.js runs in the Cloudflare Worker runtime — if incompatible, fall back to raw Innertube HTTP calls - Implement src/backend/youtube.ts with subtitle extraction via the chosen approach (youtubei.js or raw Innertube) - Parse YouTube URLs (youtube.com and youtu.be formats) to extract video ID - Implement language fallback chain: zh-CN → zh → en - Implement Webshare TCP Socket proxy via cloudflare:sockets connect() for captcha bypass - Implement hard-coded subtitle fallback from src/fallback-subtitles/demo.txt - Strip timestamp markers and clean subtitle text for AI input

step.title: AI Module & Prompt Templates (Dual Provider, Dual Mode)
step.tasks: - Implement Gemini API client as the default provider - Implement DeepSeek API client as an optional secondary provider with larger context window - Both providers expose the same capabilities: streaming article generation, structured 5W1H summary, and chapter detection - Create dual-format prompt templates supporting both providers' input formats - Implement two generation modes: chapter-only splitting (preserve original wording, add headings) when no custom rules; rule-driven generation when rules are provided - Build chapter detection prompts that split subtitle text into logical topic segments with titles and summaries - Support optional custom rule injection (task type, style, target audience, constraints) into generation prompts - Build 5W1H summarization prompt templates using chapter title plus full article context - Provider selection via URL parameter, defaulting to Gemini with DeepSeek as opt-in - Frontend model selector for switching between available providers - Graceful error handling with user-friendly messages on API failure

step.title: Session Storage with Cloudflare KV
step.tasks: - Implement src/backend/session.ts for KV read/write operations - Save session on article completion: key session:{uuid}, value { fullText, chapters[], subtitle }, TTL 3600s - Retrieve session by sessionId for 5W1H generation - Auto-expire sessions after 1 hour to prevent storage bloat

step.title: Worker Router & API Endpoints
step.tasks: - Implement request router: serve the HTML page at the root path and static assets from the public directory - Implement chapter detection endpoint: subtitle extraction followed by AI chapter boundary detection, returning chapter list with titles and summaries - Implement article generation endpoint: route to chapter-only splitting (no rule) or full generation (rule provided) based on request; return streaming response with session ID header - Filter content to only include user-selected chapters during generation - Implement 5W1H summary endpoint: retrieve session context, generate structured summary, return as JSON - Create the main entry point wiring the router and fetch handler - Handle CORS headers for cross-origin requests

step.title: Frontend: UI, Streaming Client & Markdown Rendering
step.tasks: - Build HTML page with input area: YouTube URL field, custom rules textarea (optional), model selector, and single "生成文章" button - Apply design system tokens consistently: primary blue for buttons, white background, dark text, light borders - Typography: system sans-serif font at readable base size with comfortable line height - Implement streaming client that connects to the generation endpoint and appends content to the page in real-time - Build lightweight Markdown parser to detect title, chapter headings, and paragraph structure - Render chapters as h2 headings with natural line breaks — no bordered chapter boxes - After streaming completes, inject 5W1H buttons next to each chapter heading - Build collapsible summary boxes that expand on click and show structured Who/What/When/Where/Why/How - Disable the generate button during active generation; show loading spinner and blinking cursor during streaming - Validate YouTube URL format on input with instant inline feedback

step.title: Integration Testing & End-to-End Validation
step.tasks: - Test with demo video (xRh2sVcNXQ8): subtitle extraction, chapter detection, streaming output, chapter rendering - Test default mode (no rules): verify original wording is kept, only chapter headings are added - Test custom rule mode: verify the system rewrites content following user requirements - Test custom generation rules: verify output reflects task type, style, audience, and constraints - Test 5W1H summaries: verify correct structured output and no client re-transmission of full article - Test fallback chain: simulate direct fetch failure, proxy retry, hard-coded fallback - Test error cases: invalid URL, missing subtitles, API key errors, session store unavailability - Verify responsive layout on mobile and desktop viewports

step.title: Deployment & Go-Live
step.tasks: - Create Cloudflare KV namespace via wrangler kv:namespace create - Configure Gemini API key and Webshare proxy credentials as secrets - Build frontend assets via node build.mjs - Run wrangler deploy to publish Worker to production - Verify public HTTPS access with no CORS errors - Test full flow on deployed URL with demo video

success_criteria.functionality: - YouTube URL parsing handles both youtube.com and youtu.be formats correctly - Subtitle extraction works end-to-end with the demo video (xRh2sVcNXQ8) - Subtitle language fallback chain works: zh-CN to zh to en - Chapter detection endpoint returns detected chapter list with titles and summaries - Default mode (no custom rules): article preserves original subtitle wording, detects topic boundaries, merges same-topic content into chapters, and formats dialogue with speaker name prefixes (e.g., "Jen: content") - Custom rule mode (rules provided): generated article strictly follows user requirements - Streaming article generation produces properly formatted output in real-time - Custom generation rules (task type, style, audience, constraints) observably influence output - Chapter headings (##) are rendered as h2 with natural line breaks, no border boxes - 5W1H action buttons are injected next to each chapter heading after article completion - 5W1H summaries return correct Who/What/When/Where/Why/How for each chapter - 5W1H endpoint uses server-side session context — no client re-transmission of full article - Hard-coded subtitle fallback loads correctly when direct and proxy fetch both fail - URL format validation shows instant inline feedback for invalid inputs - Generate button is disabled during active generation to prevent duplicate submissions
success_criteria.user_experience: - Single-click flow: user enters URL, optionally sets custom rules, clicks "生成文章" — everything happens in one step - Streaming text renders in real-time with typewriter effect — chunks appear as they are generated - Chapters are styled minimally: h2 headings with natural line breaks between sections - 5W1H summary boxes are collapsed by default; expand smoothly on click, collapsible again - Article layout is clean and readable: clear distinction between headings, paragraphs, and chapters - Responsive layout adapts to mobile, tablet, and desktop screen widths - Loading state shown during generation with spinner; blinking cursor during streaming - Clear error messages on failure; app remains functional for retry - After generation completes, page refresh can still re-query 5W1H summaries via KV (session survives refresh)
success_criteria.technical_quality: - Clean module separation: each src/backend/*.ts file has a single responsibility - AI API keys and Webshare credentials stored as secrets — never exposed to client - Cloudflare KV session cache auto-expires after 1 hour (TTL 3600s) - SSE streaming uses TransformStream — efficient, backpressure-aware, no memory accumulation - Subtitle fallback chain is resilient: direct → proxy → hard-coded; service never fully breaks - AI provider API failures return meaningful error messages, not raw stack traces - Frontend JS bundled via esbuild with no framework dependencies - TCP Socket proxy implementation correctly uses cloudflare:sockets connect() - No CORS errors on deployed Worker - All TypeScript code is type-safe with no `any` types except at system boundaries - Two generation modes cleanly separated: chapter-only splitting (no rewriting) and custom rule generation - Chapter detection reliably identifies logical topic segments in subtitle text
success_criteria.design_polish: - Design system tokens applied consistently: #2563eb primary, #ffffff background, #111827 text, #e5e7eb borders - Typography: system-ui sans-serif at 16px base, 1.8 line-height for comfortable reading - Input elements: rounded borders, light gray background, visible focus highlight ring - Buttons: hover state change, clear disabled state (grayed out + not-allowed cursor) - Chapters: natural h2 headings with comfortable top margin to separate sections - 5W1H summary boxes: light gray background distinct from chapter content - Smooth transitions on summary box expand/collapse and button hover - Excellent color contrast for accessibility (dark text on light background)
